sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Betis have shown inconsistent form in their last 5 matches with 1 win, 3 draws, and 1 loss, scoring 6 goals and conceding 6. Real Sociedad are in poorer form, with no wins in their last 6 matches, 2 draws, and 4 losses, scoring only 4 goals while conceding 6.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head encounters, each team has won one match, with Real Betis winning 3-0 at home and Real Sociedad winning 2-0 at home. Historically, matches between these teams tend to have under 2.5 goals, suggesting tight, low-scoring games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Real Betis rank 10th with a balanced attack and defense (6 goals scored and conceded), excelling in big chances created (2nd in the league). Real Sociedad, sitting 17th, struggle offensively with just 4 goals and are defensively vulnerable, conceding first in 4 of their last 5 games.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Real Betis have a slight edge due to playing at home and their superior league position and form. Real Sociedad’s inability to keep clean sheets (4 games without one) and poor away form could be exploited by Betis’ attacking potential, though their own defensive lapses may allow Sociedad a chance to score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Real Betis and Real Sociedad at Estadio de la Cartuja ended with a 1-1 scoreline, reflecting a closely contested battle. Both teams managed to find the back of the net early, with Cucho Hernández opening the scoring for Betis and Brais Méndez quickly equalizing for Sociedad. The game has been balanced in terms of possession and opportunities, setting the stage for an intriguing second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad held a slight edge in possession and showed more intent in the final third with a higher number of entries and penalty area penetrations. They also created more shots on target and earned several corner kicks, indicating a marginally more aggressive approach. On the other hand, Real Betis were competitive in duels, winning a good percentage of both aerial and ground challenges. Their defensive structure held firm despite Sociedad's pressure, and they managed to capitalize on a key moment to score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: For Real Betis, Cucho Hernández stood out with his early goal, proving to be a constant threat upfront. Giovani Lo Celso also played a pivotal role with an assist and several key passes, driving the midfield creativity. On the Real Sociedad side, Brais Méndez was the standout performer, not only scoring the equalizer but also dictating play with incisive passes. Mikel Oyarzabal and Takefusa Kubo showed flashes of danger, though they couldn’t convert their opportunities in the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Real Betis started brightly with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes, leading to their early goal. However, momentum shifted towards Real Sociedad shortly after, as they responded with a sustained period of pressure that resulted in their equalizer. Towards the latter stages of the half, Sociedad maintained longer spells of attacking intent, while Betis had sporadic bursts but struggled to regain dominance in the attacking third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Disciplinary concerns
halftimeSections.text: Discipline has been a concern in this half, particularly for Real Sociedad, who picked up multiple yellow cards, indicating a more physical approach. Real Betis also had a couple of bookings, which could impact their defensive organization in the second half if key players tread cautiously. Both teams will need to manage their aggression to avoid further sanctions that might tilt the balance of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Real Sociedad appear to have the upper hand based on their first-half dominance in possession and territorial play. They are likely to push for a winning goal by exploiting the flanks and utilizing the creativity of players like Brais Méndez. Real Betis, however, will not go down without a fight, and their ability to counterattack through players like Cucho Hernández could prove decisive. Expect a tight contest, with both teams likely to create scoring opportunities, potentially leading to at least one more goal.
